title: Conan O’Brien to deliver Harvard’s commencement address
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Conan O'Brien, a Harvard graduate and Brookline native, will deliver Harvard's commencement address on May 28.
O'Brien will receive an honorary degree at the commencement ceremony, praised for his humor and cultural contributions.
The comedian's prominence includes writing for 'SNL', 'The Simpsons', and his award-winning late-night TV hosting career.

title: ‘What do they have up their sleeve?’: Ian Rapoport forecasts ‘fascinating’ Patriots offseason
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Patriots release WR Stefon Diggs, creating $16.8 million in cap space, amid speculation of offseason plans.
Ian Rapoport suggests Patriots may trade for a wide receiver like A.J. Brown, enhancing their offensive lineup.
NFL free agency begins March 11, with Patriots potentially making significant moves after clearing cap space.

title: Watch Stephen Colbert and Steve Carell auction off props from ‘Late Show’ set before cancellation
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Stephen Colbert and Steve Carell auctioned off 'Late Show' props before the show's final episode on May 21.
Proceeds from the auction, featuring items like a replica sword and signed photos, will benefit World Central Kitchen.
Carell reminisced about his comedy roots with Colbert and discussed personal stories during the 'Late Show' segment.

title: These were Boston’s most popular dog names in 2025
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Luna was the most popular dog name in Boston in 2025, followed by Charlie and Bella.
Labrador and Golden retrievers were the top registered dog breeds in Boston last year.
Boston dogs over six months must be licensed by March 31, with fines for non-compliance.

title: Another wintry mix? Here’s what meteorologists are saying about the timing and impacts.
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: A wintry mix of rain, freezing rain, sleet, and snow is expected from Thursday night into Friday morning.
The greatest risk for ice is in the Southern Worcester Hills and Southern Berkshires; significant sleet likely further north.
This weather system will likely affect the Thursday evening and Friday morning commutes in southern New England.

title: One of the best brewery tours in America is in New England, according to USA Today readers
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Allagash Brewing Company in Portland, Maine ranked No. 6 among the 10 best brewery tours in America's USA Today list.
The Allagash tours range from a 30-minute Taste of Allagash tour to an hour-long Signature Beer & Barrels Experience.
Schilling Beer Co. in NH and Hill Farmstead Brewery in VT were also recognized among America's best brewpubs and beer gardens.

title: Foul play suspected after human remains found in Shirley, police say
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Human remains found near Maritime Veterans Memorial Bridge in Shirley; foul play is suspected.
Discovery made by young people Wednesday evening; police confirmed presence of body parts in the water.
State Police and dive team assisting in investigation; increased police presence expected in the area.

title: Boston community grapples with fear and protest after U.S.-Israeli strike in Iran
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Boston protests erupted following a U.S.-Israeli strike that killed Iran’s Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.
Activist Joe Tache argues that wartime spending harms U.S. domestic priorities like health care and education.
Massachusetts Peace Action supports legislation to limit presidential war powers, co-sponsored by Senators Markey and Warren.

title: 5 best new restaurant openings and dining updates in Boston (March 5, 2026)
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: LPM Restaurant will open its first Northeast location in Boston's Back Bay, offering a French-Mediterranean menu.
Cafe Noodo, a new hand-pulled noodle shop, has opened near TD Garden with a menu featuring various noodle soups.
Call Me Honey has opened at the site of former Curio Coffee, offering coffee, liège waffles, and guest roasters' products.

title: 3 reasons for optimism and 3 causes for concern as the Red Sox approach a new season
author: Trevor Hass
bullets: Roman Anthony stands out as a rising star for the Red Sox, aiming to boost his performance this season.
Veteran pitcher Garrett Crochet leads a bolstered rotation with newcomers like Ranger Suárez and Sonny Gray.
Participation in the World Baseball Classic offers players momentum but raises concerns about potential injuries.
